chunks.title: Intelligent recognition algorithm of connection relation of substation secondary wiring drawing based on D-LLE algorithm
chunks.text:
## Research

## Intelligent recognition algorithm of connection relation of substation secondary wiring drawing based on D-LLE algorithm

Hongzhi Liu 1 · Bojie Yang 1 · Fang Kang 1 · Qian Li 1 · Hongyang Zhang 1

Received: 14 August 2024 / Accepted: 12 December 2024

Published online: 21 December 2024

© The Author(s) 2024, corrected publication 2025

OPEN

## Abstract

The accurate recognition of the connection relationship of the substation secondary wiring drawings is a key link in the operation and maintenance of the power system, but the traditional manual recognition method has the problems of time-consuming, labor-intensive and error-prone. Therefore, this paper proposes an intelligent recognition algorithm for substation secondary wiring drawings based on D-LLE algorithm. The composition of the substation secondary layout diagram is analyzed, and the connection relationship feature image is preprocessed, including smoothing and contrast enhancement, to improve the image quality. D-LLE algorithm (Denoised Locally Linear Embedding) is used to effectively extract the connection features in the drawings, which improves the feature expression ability and recognition accuracy. The improved Canny operator is used to extract the feature edge of the connection relationship, and the feature recognition algorithm is used to realize the intelligent recognition of the connection relationship. The experimental results show that the completeness of connection information extraction of substation secondary wiring drawings obtained by the proposed algorithm is between 98.56% and 99.98%, and the mean absolute error of intelligent recognition drawings connection relationship is always lower than 0.01. It shows that the proposed algorithm can accurately and quickly identify the connection relationship in the substation secondary wiring drawings, significantly improve the recognition efficiency, and has strong robustness and versatility, which can provide strong support for the intelligent operation and maintenance of the power system.

## Article Highlights

1. The composition of substation secondary configuration diagram is analyzed, and the pretreatment technology is proposed.
2. The D-LLE algorithm is introduced to extract features, which improves the recognition accuracy of connection relations.
3. The Canny operator is improved for edge extraction, and intelligent recognition of connection relationship is realized.

Keywords D-LLE algorithm · Substation · Secondary wiring drawings · Connection relation · Intelligent recognition · Algorithm design

* Hongzhi Liu, zhi8589660424@163.com | 1 State Grid Shandong Electric Power Company Economic & Technology Research Institute, Jinan 250021, Shandong, China.

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

Vol.:(0123456789)

## 1 Introduction

With the rapid development of power system, substation as a key node of power transmission and distribution, its safe and stable operation is very important. As an important technical document to guide on-site operation and maintenance, the complexity and fineness of substation secondary wiring drawings are increasing day by day [1]. The traditional manual review and identification method is difficult to meet the actual needs of efficiency and accuracy. Secondary wiring drawings contain a lot of electrical connection information. The accurate identification of this information is of great significance for fault troubleshooting, system optimization and safety evaluation. However, the diversity and complexity of drawings, such as a wide variety of symbols, dense and irregular layout, bring great challenges to manual recognition [2]. Therefore, it is particularly important to study an algorithm that can intellitively identify the connection relationship of substation secondary wiring drawings.

chunks.title: Intelligent recognition algorithm of connection relation of substation secondary wiring drawing based on D-LLE algorithm
chunks.text:
Vol.:(0123456789)

Currently, better methods have been developed for the identification of drawings. For example, Cheng et al. [3] proposed a method to detect the diagram symbols of plant and station connections based on the double-layer and block detection networks. In this method, 11 typical elements such as circuit breakers, isolating switches, reactors, grounding cutters, etc. are identified after the wiring diagram of the electric plant is cut according to electrical logic. The dual-block detection network is a composed of a key area detection layer based on the Area-YOLOV5 network and a specific element identification layer based on the Obj-YOLOv5 network. Zhou et al. [4] proposed a feature recognition and matching method for shaft part drawings based on contour detection. Through binarization, open and close operation, Canny contour detection, Hough circle detection and other operations of the drawing, the rectangular contour and circular contour in the drawing are extracted and identified. The matching is completed by analyzing the size and relative position relationship between each contour and the surrounding dimensions. Wang et al. [5] proposed a new method for overlapping character recognition of building construction drawings based on adaptive scale edge features. The overlapping character regions are preliminarily determined based on the spatial distribution characteristics of pixels, and the adaptive scale edge features are defined and extracted. With the help of bivariate matching probability function, the result combination of 'position + content' is screened, and the global optimal principle is used instead of the absolute threshold as the recognition standard, and the correct recognition result is finally output. Shoba et al. [6] proposed an adaptive total recursive deep neural learning technique to improve accuracy with minimal time complexity. Feature extraction and classification are the two processes included in the proposed technique. The adaptive total recursive deep neural learning technique collects face images from a database and performs three-layer training on the face images.

However, there are also some problems in the above methods. For example, the double-layer and block detection network in reference [3] has a large demand for training data and is sensitive to parameter adjustment. The contour detection method of literature [4] will cause unclear edges due to noise interference in complex background. In reference [5], the adaptive scale edge feature will become unstable when the image scale changes significantly. The adaptive fully recurrent deep neural learning technology applied in the method of reference [6] will lead to the decrease of training and inference speed due to the increase of network structure complexity. These methods still face common problems: large demand for training data, sensitivity to complex background noise, unstable features, and slow training and inference speed caused by network complexity.

To solve these problems, the D-LLE algorithm is introduced in this paper. As an efficient machine learning algorithm, the D-LLE algorithm can use its powerful feature extraction and classification ability to quickly and accurately identify the connection relationship in the drawings, improve the recognition efficiency and accuracy, and provide a strong guarantee for the safe and stable operation of the power system. Therefore, an intelligent recognition algorithm based on D-LLE algorithm for substation secondary wiring drawings connection relationship is proposed.

chunks.title: Intelligent recognition algorithm of connection relation of substation secondary wiring drawing based on D-LLE algorithm
chunks.text:
Vol.:(0123456789)

The proposed method deeply analyzed the composition of the substation secondary layout diagram, and clarified the key elements and connection relationships in the drawing. The image preprocessing was carried out for the connection relationship features to highlight the connection relationship features. The D-LLE algorithm was used to extract the connection features of drawings, which effectively captured the connection features in drawings. The improved Canny operator was used to extract the connection relationship feature edges to improve the accuracy and robustness of edge detection. Through the connection relationship feature recognition algorithm, the intelligent recognition of the substation secondary wiring diagram connection relationship is realized. By optimizing the feature extraction strategy, introducing a lightweight network structure and enhancing the generalization ability of the model, the proposed method aims to effectively reduce data dependence, improve anti-noise performance, stabilize feature expression, and accelerate the training and inference process.

<!-- image -->

## 2 Intelligent recognition algorithm of connection relation of substation secondary wiring drawing

## 2.1 Composition analysis of substation secondary wiring drawings

Substation secondary wiring drawings are necessary for the design, commissioning and maintenance of power plants and substation and distribution stations. The location, function, and connection of these elements in a drawing are critical for understanding the operation of the overall system. An in-depth analysis of the composition of drawings can help researchers better understand the basic structure and elements of drawings, and provide a solid foundation for subsequent intelligent recognition research. The connection relation information of the substation secondary wiring drawing mainly includes the following two parts:

Graphic symbol: In the secondary wiring diagram, various secondary devices, such as relays [7], measuring instruments, automatic devices, etc., are represented by specific graphical symbols. These graphical symbols are not only used to identify different devices, but can also clearly show the connection between devices.

Text symbol: In addition to graphical symbols, the secondary wiring diagram also uses text symbols to indicate the name, function, and connection status of the device. These text symbols help engineers to quickly understand drawings, identify connections between devices, and understand how the entire secondary circuit works.

In addition, a title bar and a specification sheet are included. The title bar is usually located at the top or bottom of the drawing and is used to provide basic information about the drawing. It mainly includes the following contents: 1 Drawing name: Clearly mark the drawing as a substation secondary wiring diagram for easy reference and identification; 2 Project name or station name: Marking the name of the substation or project to which the drawing belongs helps to match the drawing with the actual project; 3 Drawing number: Each drawing has a unique number for easy management and lookup; and 4 Design unit, design date and other information: Mark the design unit and design date of the drawings to reflect the source and timeliness of the drawings.
chunks.section_path: Research > 2.1 Composition analysis of substation secondary wiring drawings

chunks.title: Intelligent recognition algorithm of connection relation of substation secondary wiring drawing based on D-LLE algorithm
chunks.text:
Text symbol: In addition to graphical symbols, the secondary wiring diagram also uses text symbols to indicate the name, function, and connection status of the device. These text symbols help engineers to quickly understand drawings, identify connections between devices, and understand how the entire secondary circuit works.

A detailed sheet is usually located on the side or bottom of the drawing and is used to list all the equipment, components and related information used in the drawing. It mainly includes the following contents: 1 Device or component name: List the names of all equipment or components used in the drawing, such as relays, measuring instruments, switches, etc.; 2 Model specifications: Label the type and specifications of the equipment or components so that they can be accurately matched during procurement and installation; 3 Quantity: Listing the amount of each device or component used in the drawing helps with statistics and accounting; and 4 Note information: Additional remarks can be added, such as special installation requirements, precautions, etc.

## 2.2 Connection relation feature image preprocessing

The composition analysis of substation secondary wiring drawings is the basis for understanding the content and structure of drawings, clarifying the various elements contained in drawings (such as various secondary equipment), the functions of equipment and the potential connection relationships between devices, providing important prior knowledge for subsequent extraction of the characteristics of the connection relationships of drawings. However, the connection relationships in the substation secondary wiring drawings are usually complex, containing a large number of lines, symbols and text information, which requires pre-processing of the drawings, such as smoothing light processing and contrast enhancement, so as to help extract key connection relationship features and reduce the complexity of subsequent recognition.

## 2.2.1 Image smoothing processing

Features such as lines and intersections are very important for the subsequent recognition and analysis of the connection relation feature images. However, the brightness or contrast of some areas of the original connection relation feature image is insufficient because of uneven illumination and shadows, which makes it difficult to accurately identify and extract the key features in the image. Even light processing can adjust the brightness distribution of the image, making the brightness of the image more uniform, so as to improve the quality of the image. The proposed method uses a mask to balance the overall light intensity of the images of substation secondary wiring drawings [8, 9]. /u1D6FC ( i , j ) represents the original image, /u1D6FC b ( i , j ) represents the background image obtained after filtering by Gaussian filtering, /u1D6FC r ( i , j ) represents the image before linear stretching, ( i , j ) represents any pixel in the image, and /u1D6FE represents the grayscale offset, then the following relationship exists:

<!-- image -->

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

The pixel value of /u1D6FC r ( i , j ) is stretched in the range of [0,255], with /u1D6FF representing the linear stretch factor. The image /u1D6FC m ( i , j ) after obtaining uniform light is shown as follows:

## 2.2.2 Image contrast enhancement processing

Uniform light processing is mainly concerned with the adjustment of the overall brightness of the connected feature image to eliminate the phenomenon of uneven brightness. When there are obvious brightness changes or shadows in the image, excessive or inappropriate homogenizing light processing leads to the loss or blurring of some important details in the connected relation feature image, which has a negative impact on the subsequent feature extraction. Contrast enhancement processing can stretch the gray level range of the image after homogenizing light processing, making the image more contrast, especially when processing images with complex structures and details. The proposed method uses a gray-level corrosion method to enhance the image contrast [10-12].

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

/u1D703 ( i ′ , j ′ ) is used to represent the structural element ( i ′ , j ′ ) is used to represent any pixel in the structural element, and the image /u1D6FC e ( i , j ) after gray corrosion enhanced contrast is shown as follows:

## 2.3 Feature extraction of drawing connection relationship based on D-LLE algorithm

The original substation secondary wiring drawing contains a large amount of information and details, but not all of the information is helpful for connection identification. For substation secondary wiring drawings, the characteristics of the connection relationship are often reflected in the brightness differences between different lines, symbols or components. The brightness contrast of the image can be adjusted and the brightness range of the image can be stretched to make it more uniform, so as to eliminate the interference of this non-uniformity on feature extraction and highlight the features of the connection relationship. Therefore, the connection relation features of the preprocessed substation secondary wiring drawings are extracted, and the key features related to the connection relation are screened to improve the calculation efficiency. Locally Linear Embedding (LLE) is a nonlinear dimensionality reduction algorithm designed to map high-dimensional data into a low-dimensional space to maintain a locally linear relationship between data points. D-LLE algorithm (Denoised Locally Linear Embedding) is an improved version of LLE that improves the robustness to noise by introducing a denoising mechanism. The algorithm first calculates the linear relationship between each data point and its neighboring points through local linear reconstruction, and then reconstructs these relationships in the low-dimensional space to finally achieve the dimensionality reduction of the data [13, 14]. In this way, the D-LLE algorithm can effectively maintain the local characteristics of the data and reduce the impact of dimension disaster in the process of dimensionality reduction, so as to accurately extract the characteristics of the connection relation of the drawing and optimize the recognition effect of the drawing.

## 2.4 Intelligent identification of connection relation of substation secondary wiring drawing

## 2.4.1 Improved Canny operator connection relation feature edge extraction

In substation secondary wiring drawings, the connection relationship is usually reflected in the intersection and connection between different lines, equipment or symbols. These converging and connecting points usually correspond to the edges of the image. The edge of the drawing is extracted by using the features of connection relationships obtained in Sect. 2.3, and the position and shape of these connection relationships are clearly displayed, providing a clear basis for the subsequent identification of connection relationships. The improved Canny operator [15] was used to extract the feature edge of the connection relation in the substation secondary wiring drawing image.

The traditional Canny operator usually uses a Gaussian smoothing filter as the filtering function. Although the calculation is simple, the filter has no filtering effect on the nonlinear noise. The filtering ability is directly affected by the standard deviation /u1D70E , and the first-order difference of the two directions in the traditional Canny operator will cause the loss of part of the image information, and the traditional Canny operator adopts a manually defined threshold, which will lead to a certain deviation in the result. Therefore, the proposed method improves upon the traditional Canny operator.

In the proposed method, a nonlinear median filter [16] is used to replace the Gaussian filter as the filtering algorithm. Median filtering arranges the pixel values of the substation secondary wiring drawing image in ascending order and selects the median value to implement filtering.

Then the 8-direction template of Sobel operator [17] is introduced to calculate the image gradient. Assume that there is an image f ( i , j ) of the substation secondary wiring drawing, and ( i , j ) represents the value of any pixel in the image. Then the directional gradients /u1D71B i and /u1D71B j obtained by the Sobel operator are as follows:

<!-- image -->

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Finally, Otsu method is used to replace the manual threshold selection. It is assumed that there are p i = gray levels in the images of the substation secondary wiring drawings, and the occurrence times of each gray level are denoted as mi . For an image of substation secondary wiring drawing with size M × N , the occurrence probability P i of the i gray level is calculated as follows:

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

Threshold K is set to divide the background and edge region of the image [18], based on which the probability P b and P o of the background and edge region in the image are calculated as follows:

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

The mean values /u1D707 b and /u1D707 o and variances /u1D70E 2 b and /u1D70E 2 o of background and edge pixels are obtained by combining the calculation results of Eq. (17), as shown below:

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

chunks.text:
<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

/u1D707 represents the global pixel mean and calculates the interclass variance /u1D70E 2 B between the background region and the edge region, as follows:

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

The threshold K is constantly adjusted in the range [0,Y-1], and the inter-class variance under different thresholds is repeatedly calculated. Among them, the threshold corresponding to the maximum value of the inter-class variance is the desired threshold, which can distinguish the feature edge region of the connection relation from the background region in the image of the substation secondary wiring drawing, and realize the feature edge extraction of the connection relation.

<!-- image -->

## 2.4.2 Identification of connection relationship features

Though edge detection, the edge information in the image (that is, the place where the gray value changes dramatically) is extracted. This edge information usually corresponds to the connection relationships in substation secondary wiring drawings, such as the connection points and crossing points of the lines. Even under poor lighting conditions or image quality, it can maintain a good recognition effect. The region growth method combines adjacent pixels with similar properties into one region to realize the feature recognition of connection relations. The proposed method formulated growth rules according to gray threshold, denoting gray value of growing point as p , gray value of pixels in the neighborhood of growing point 8 as q i , i ∈ [ 1, 2, ⋯ , 8 ] , and growth threshold as T d , then:

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

The main process of dynamically identifying the connection relation characteristics of substation secondary wiring drawing images by region growth method is as follows:

- 1 In the rough recognition area, the feature edge points of the connection relation of the substation secondary wiring drawing image extracted by the improved Canny operator are set as seed points.
- 2 Set the seed point growth rule, and determine the growth threshold according to formula (20);
- 3 Take the seed point as the starting growth point, and mark the region where the seed point is located as the target region Q ;
- 4 All pixel points in the neighborhood of growing point 8 are searched, and the pixels that do not belong to Q and do not exceed the coarse recognition area are retained.
- 5 By comparing retained pixel points and corresponding growth points, pixel points that meet the growth rules are recorded as new growth points and added to Q ;
- 6 If the number of new growth points is 0, the algorithm ends, and the final region Q is the feature region of the connection relation of the substation secondary wiring drawing image. Otherwise, return to Step 4 and repeat until termination requirements are met.

Based on the identified connection characteristics of the drawings, AI image deep learning technology [19] is used to conduct consistency and normative testing of the drawings involved in the secondary loop (CAD, PDF, pictures, paper and other formats). Its flow chart is shown in Fig. 1, in order to achieve the machine to replace part of the human work, to achieve the purpose of reducing the workload, while improving the quality of check work.
chunks.section_path: Research > 2.4.2 Identification of connection relationship features

chunks.title: Intelligent recognition algorithm of connection relation of substation secondary wiring drawing based on D-LLE algorithm
chunks.text:
Based on the identified connection characteristics of the drawings, AI image deep learning technology [19] is used to conduct consistency and normative testing of the drawings involved in the secondary loop (CAD, PDF, pictures, paper and other formats). Its flow chart is shown in Fig. 1, in order to achieve the machine to replace part of the human work, to achieve the purpose of reducing the workload, while improving the quality of check work.

Step 1: Collect historical drawings of electrical secondary circuits according to industry needs;

Step 2: According to the historical power drawings, collect and analyze the historical power drawings;

Step 3: The drawings in the substation are statistically classified according to the drawing method and the affiliated design institute, and the historical power drawings are marked according to the drawing type and/or drawing method. Step 4:Redundantly cut drawings;

Step 5: The above datasets are labeled as unit information bar, general drawing connection relation corner, special drawing A connection pin, etc. Parameters suitable for operation detection are obtained by k-means clustering on classified datasets, and target detection is carried out based on the YOLO improved target detection algorithm [20]. The best image detection model is trained, and the best text detection model is trained based on OCR recognition algorithm.

Step 6: The text recognition algorithm is used to recognize the classified power drawings and obtain the text data of the drawings. The classified power drawings are detected by the target detection algorithm, and the data of connecting lines and electrical components of drawings are obtained.

Step 7: Based on the pre-training model, the text data of the drawing, the connection line data of the drawing and the electrical component data of the drawing are trained to obtain the text detection model and the graph detection model of different drawing types and/or drawing modes.

Step 8: According to the object detection module, the power drawing to be detected is analyzed and text information, electrical component information and connection line information contained in the power drawing are extracted.

Step 9: Power drawing ledger data are generated according to the text information, electrical components and connection line information.

<!-- image -->

Fig. 1 Intelligent identification flow chart of secondary loop connection relation

Fig. 2. 110 kV substation line protection secondary wiring diagram

## 3 Experimental design and result analysis

To verify the validity of the intelligent identification method for the connection relation of substation secondary wiring drawings, the following experiments are conducted. Consider the secondary wiring diagram for line protection of 110 kV substation as an example (as shown in Fig. 2). The drawing shows a typical line protection device, with a set of secondary windings of CT and PT connected to the line protection device, control switches for manual control and protection devices. The equipment information and connection relationships were extracted from the drawings using research methods, literature [4] method and literature [5] method.

The integrity data of the extraction of the connection relation feature information of the substation secondary wiring drawings obtained through the experiments are shown in Table 1.

As shown in the data in Table 1, the completeness of the extraction of connection relation information of the substation secondary wiring drawing obtained by the proposed algorithm ranges from 98.56% to 99.98%.

ROC curves can be used to measure the accuracy of the identification of connection relationships in method drawings. The larger the area between the ROC curve and the horizontal and vertical coordinates, that is, the larger the AUC value, the higher the identification accuracy of the connection relation of the corresponding method. The test results for the proposed method, the method [3] and the method [4] are shown in Fig. 3:

As shown in Fig. 3, the proposed algorithm has the same false-positive rate. The true positive rate is higher than that of method [3] and the method [4], indicating that the points detected by the proposed method have the highest proportion of real drawing connection relation points, and the AUC value of the proposed algorithm is higher than that of the method [3] and the method [4].

To comprehensively evaluate the performance of drawing connection recognition, the proposed method introduced the average absolute error to further test the reliability and accuracy of the proposed method, the method [3] and the method [4].

M and N are used to represent image length and width, ( x , y ) is used to represent arbitrary pixel value, G ( x , y ) is used to represent real pixel value, and H ( x , y ) is used to represent pixel value detected by different methods. The average absolute error MAE is calculated as follows:

chunks.title: Intelligent recognition algorithm of connection relation of substation secondary wiring drawing based on D-LLE algorithm
chunks.text:
Table 2 Results of average absolute error detection

<!-- formula-not-decoded -->

According to formula (11), the average absolute error of the recognition results of the three methods in the feature categories of the connection relations in the images of the secondary wiring drawings of different substations is calculated, as shown in Table 2:

It can be seen from the data in the table that the proposed method has excellent performance in the recognition of various connection modes, which is significantly better than the other two methods. Specifically, the average absolute error of the proposed method is 0.0022, 0.0095, 0.0036, 0.0082 and 0.0088 among the current loop connection, voltage loop connection, switch input connection, control loop connection and signal loop connection, respectively. Both of them are much lower than the error values of the methods in literature [3] and [4]. For example, in the current loop connection, the error of the proposed method is only 0.0022, while the error of the method [3] and the method [4] are 0.0429 and 0.0225, respectively. Similarly, in the switching input connection, the error of the proposed method is 0.0036, which is much lower than the 0.0414 of the method [3] and 0.0457 of the method [4]. These data fully show that the proposed method can identify the connection relation feature categories in substation secondary wiring drawings more accurately, and has higher recognition accuracy and robustness. This is due to the advantages of D-LLE algorithm in feature extraction and the accuracy of improved Canny operator in edge extraction. Therefore, the proposed method provides a new and effective means for intelligent processing and analysis of substation secondary wiring drawings, and has important practical application value.

## 4 Conclusion

In order to improve the efficiency and accuracy of substation secondary wiring drawings connection recognition, and then provide strong support for the intelligent operation and maintenance of power system, this paper studies the intelligent recognition algorithm of substation secondary wiring drawings connection based on D-LLE algorithm. The composition of the substation secondary configuration diagram is deeply analyzed, and the connection relationship feature image is preprocessed. The D-LLE algorithm is used to effectively extract the connection features in the drawings, and the improved Canny operator is used to extract the edge of connection features, and the feature recognition algorithm is used to realize the intelligent recognition of connection relations. The experimental results show that the completeness of connection information extraction of substation secondary wiring drawings obtained by the proposed algorithm is between 98.56% and 99.98%, and the mean absolute error of intelligent recognition drawings connection relationship is always lower than 0.01, indicating that the proposed algorithm can accurately and quickly identify the connection relationship in substation secondary wiring drawings. The recognition efficiency is significantly improved. However, there are some limitations in this study, such as the recognition accuracy of the algorithm still needs to be improved for particularly complex or fuzzy drawings. Looking forward to the future, we will continue to optimize the algorithm, improve its ability to recognize complex drawings, and further expand its application scope.

chunks.title: Intelligent recognition algorithm of connection relation of substation secondary wiring drawing based on D-LLE algorithm

According to formula (11), the average absolute error of the recognition results of the three methods in the feature categories of the connection relations in the images of the secondary wiring drawings of different substations is calculated, as shown in Table 2:

Author contributions Hongzhi Liu: Conceptualization, Writing - Original Draft, Writing - Review and Editing, Project administration, Visualization, Bojie Yang: Data Curation, Writing - Original Draft, Formal Analysis, Methodology, Fang Kang: Investigation, Visualization, Writing Original Draft Qian Li: Resources, Supervision, Writing - Original Draft Hongyang Zhang: Software, Validation, Writing - Original Draft.
